message:
test(fcp-core): pin HolderProof signable-bytes + serde format invariants (flywheel_connectors-bc6x4)

Bead asks for CapabilityProof Display + roundtrip. fcp-core has no
type literally named CapabilityProof; the capability-binding proof
is HolderProof (protocol.rs:307), the Ed25519 signature proving the
requesting node holds the capability token. HolderProof does NOT
implement Display, so the bead's "Display formatting" ask has no
analogue. Existing invoke_golden_vectors::holder_proof covers
basic surface (domain separator presence, determinism, per-field
distinctness). This complement pins the format-contract gaps:

- Domain separator (b"FCP2-HOLDER-PROOF-V1") at exact head — first
  20 bytes, value pinned with a regression-loud assertion since
  any drift silently invalidates every existing signature
- Length prefix walk: u32 little-endian at documented offsets,
  prefixes match actual field byte length, no trailing bytes
- Length-prefix injectivity: shifting one byte from request_id to
  operation_id (or operation_id to jti) MUST produce different
  bytes — collision-prevention guarantee
- Empty fields encode as zero-length prefix with no trailing bytes
- JSON wire shape: signature as 128-char lowercase hex
  (hex_or_bytes serde), holder_node as canonical id string
- JSON round-trip preserves both fields; over/undersized signature
  rejected
- CBOR round-trip preserves both fields
- CBOR signature MUST be byte string of length 64 (not hex Text)
  — pin hex_or_bytes serde swap behavior
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode to same proof
- signable_bytes is independent of HolderProof field values (pure
  function of req_id / op_id / jti only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
